Internal header for memory allocator routines, including a function to free unused memory.

# Purpose
This C header file, `jmem_allocator_internal.h`, is part of the internal memory allocation component for a software system. It defines an interface for internal routines related to memory management, specifically for freeing unused memory. The file includes a preprocessor directive to ensure that it is only used internally, as indicated by the error message if `JMEM_ALLOCATOR_INTERNAL` is not defined. The header declares an external function, [`jmem_run_free_unused_memory_callbacks`](<#jmem_run_free_unused_memory_callbacks>), which is intended to be used for running callbacks to free unused memory based on a specified severity level, size, and a boolean flag. The file includes standard headers `<stdbool.h>` and `<stddef.h>` for basic data types and definitions.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `stdbool.h`
- `stddef.h`


# Function Declarations (Public API)

---
### jmem\_run\_free\_unused\_memory\_callbacks<!-- {{#callable_declaration:jmem_run_free_unused_memory_callbacks}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/jerry-core/jmem/jmem-allocator-internal.h#L30>)

Triggers memory cleanup callbacks to free unused memory.
- **Description**: Use this function to attempt to free unused memory when memory pressure is high. It triggers registered callbacks to release memory based on the specified severity level. If the requested memory cannot be freed and `fatal_if_not_freed` is true, the program will terminate. This function is typically used in memory-constrained environments to manage memory more effectively.
- **Inputs**:
    - `severity`: Indicates the severity of the memory freeing request. It determines how aggressively the function should attempt to free memory. Valid values are defined by the `jmem_free_unused_memory_severity_t` type.
    - `requested_size_bytes`: Specifies the number of bytes that need to be freed. This value guides the memory freeing process but does not guarantee that the exact amount will be freed.
    - `fatal_if_not_freed`: A boolean flag that, if set to true, will cause the program to terminate if the requested memory cannot be freed. If false, the function will attempt to free memory without terminating the program if unsuccessful.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`jmem_run_free_unused_memory_callbacks`](<jmem-allocator.c.md#jmem_run_free_unused_memory_callbacks>)  (Implementation)
